seoTitle: How to Export Jira Backlog Issues to Excel
metaDescription: Export your entire Jira backlog, including issues and tickets, to an Excel file for reporting or offline review.
intro: The backlog can grow into hundreds of issues, and sometimes the easiest way to review it all is outside Jira in a spreadsheet. This guide covers exporting your backlog issues and tickets to Excel in one pass.

steps.heading: Open your backlog view
steps.body: Navigate to the project's backlog to see the full list of unscheduled issues and tickets.

steps.heading: Filter to the exact scope you need
steps.body: Use search filters to narrow the backlog to a specific status, label, or date range before exporting.

steps.heading: Access the export option
steps.body: From the issue search or filter results screen, find the export function near the top of the list.

steps.heading: Select Excel as the format
steps.body: Choose the Excel or CSV export type rather than PDF or Word.

steps.heading: Review the downloaded spreadsheet
steps.body: Open the file to confirm all backlog issues and tickets exported with the fields you expected.

faq.question: Does exporting the backlog include subtasks?
faq.answer: Subtasks are included if they match your search filter; otherwise they may need a separate export.

faq.question: Can I export tickets from multiple projects at once?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your search filter spans multiple projects, the export will include all matching issues.

faq.question: Will sprint-assigned issues be excluded from a backlog export?
faq.answer: It depends on your filter; a backlog-specific filter usually excludes issues already committed to an active sprint.

faq.question: Is there a faster way to export very large backlogs?
faq.answer: For very large exports, using the Jira REST API is often faster and more reliable than the manual export button.
